title: Spike: Document Identifier Mapping Across WordPress, Salesforce, and DataTools Pro
description:
Architecture spike to align on the data model and complex relationships for tracking user identifiers across three systems: WordPress (lead capture), Salesforce (CRM), and DataTools Pro (application).

## Problem
A single person can exist multiple times across systems, and email addresses may differ between Salesforce and sign-up forms. The challenge is tying the original web-to-lead form submission to the user's identity in DataTools Pro, especially when that person may not be identifiable by email alone.

## Key Relationships
- WordPress Gravity Form captures the initial lead via a one-time password (OTP) flow
- Salesforce stores the CRM record with its own org ID and user identity
- DataTools Pro maintains users and teams with their own IDs
- A junction object exists in the CRM to support multiple DataTools Pro tenants and users per lead/account

## Identity Flow
1. WordPress Gravity Form submission triggers an OTP email for validation
2. On first login, the user is created in DataTools Pro with a new user ID and team ID
3. The user ID and team ID are passed to Zapier alongside the Salesforce SFID and SFOrgID
4. Consistent public-facing IDs (same as Pendio tracking) should be used across all systems

## User Invite Scenario
When an existing user invites another without the web-to-lead form, the invite email becomes the customer email. This is pushed to the CRM when the invited user accepts the invite link.
acceptance_criteria: Data model documents the relationships between WordPress leads, Salesforce contacts, and DataTools Pro users
Identity mapping handles cases where email differs between Salesforce and sign-up form
OTP validation flow ties Gravity Form submissions to new DataTools Pro user creation
First login passes userID, teamID, SFID, and SFOrgID to Zapier
Public-facing IDs are consistent across WordPress, Salesforce, DataTools Pro, and Pendio
User invite flow pushes the invite email to CRM on acceptance
middleware_changes: Integration logic connecting WordPress Gravity Form OTP flow to DataTools Pro user creation, and passing user/team identifiers to Zapier on first login.
data_model_changes: Junction object in CRM supporting many-to-many between leads/accounts and DataTools Pro tenants/users. Identity mapping across WordPress lead IDs, Salesforce SFIDs, and DataTools Pro user/team IDs.
implementation_notes: The spike covered three integration points: (1) WordPress Gravity Form → OTP → DataTools Pro sign-up, (2) first login passing IDs to Zapier, (3) user invite email pushed to CRM. Uses the same public-facing IDs as Pendio for cross-system consistency.
comment_summary: Waqar asked for clarification on the authentication flow from WordPress to Salesforce to DataTools Pro. Ryan detailed the three-part integration: OTP-based Gravity Form sign-up, first-login ID passing to Zapier (userID, teamID, SFID, SFOrgID), and the need for consistent public-facing IDs across all systems.
